danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile sprites with distinct attack patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Areas containing active threats requiring evasion or combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Spikes, moving platforms, and environmental obstacles with contrasting colors
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental damage zones that drain health on contact

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen changes to enclosed spaces with larger enemy sprites and warning positioning
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent major threat encounter requiring focused engagement

safety_signals.visual_form: Solid platforms with clear pathways, neutral background colors
safety_signals.meaning: Traversable safe zones without immediate threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible power-ups and item pickups with distinct sprite appearance
safety_signals.meaning: Beneficial resources that restore or enhance player capability

faction_indicators.color_identity: Primary sprite coloring in bright/distinct hues
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player movement path through hostile territory

faction_indicators.color_identity: Contrasting enemy sprite colors distinct from player
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types with different behavioral animations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy placement patterns suggesting military/organized opposition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen populated with multiple animated enemy sprites, hazards highlighted
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol and attack in coordinated patterns; Strider must navigate through fire

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sparse enemy presence, clear platform geometry, reduced visual clutter
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies stationary or distant; player can move freely across space

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large enemy sprite centered on screen, dramatic arena layout with distinctive background
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Single powerful enemy with complex attack patterns; screen-filling threat

environmental_storytelling.example: Military or industrial environment design suggests organized enemy territory
Spikes and moving obstacles arranged to create challenging gauntlets reflecting hostile world state
Higher enemy concentration in certain areas indicates strategic strongholds or defended positions

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stationary or simple patrol pattern animation
Animated attack movement toward player, sprite orientation changes
Multi-frame attack animation sequences, movement between screen positions

notes: Source access limited. Strider (1990 Genesis) data extracted from available TCRF reference material; detailed visual communication mechanics require direct game analysis. Game uses classic platformer visual language: sprite-based enemy differentiation, terrain hazard visibility through color contrast, and spatial layout to communicate threat levels. World state manifest through enemy density and screen composition rather than environmental shader/weather effects (technical limitations of 1990 Genesis hardware).
